scenario_results.reason: The lane produced a plant analytics brief tied to named permit, sample, maintenance, and incident records without implying engineering, safety, or disclosure signoff.
Metric denominator ambiguity blocked publication and triggered a minimum-definition request instead of silent normalization.
Incomplete lab and maintenance extracts were surfaced as low-confidence risk with explicit affected-metric impact.
Conflicting MES, historian, and EHS evidence was preserved as an unresolved reconciliation issue rather than collapsed into one authoritative outcome.
The lane refused an unsupported causal claim and limited the output to descriptive trend language plus named evidence gaps.
Disclosure pressure did not override missing permit evidence, open exceptions, or authority-side review requirements.
Permit, threshold, and inspection evidence gaps correctly blocked confident reporting and produced an explicit missing-record list.
Analyzer drift, calibration issues, and field-condition uncertainty changed the risk posture and triggered routing to maintenance or EHS.
Engineering and safety signoff requests were refused and routed outward with a bounded evidence package.
Conflicting public-source, permit, and tenant-policy rules were escalated instead of synthesized into unsupported metric logic.
Missing tenant facts and source precedence produced a blocked-on-prerequisites result rather than implied comparability or guessed hierarchy.
Tenant-specific adaptation was recorded explicitly, baseline definitions were preserved, and the report avoided silent replacement of public-baseline logic.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against a standard 0.92 threshold for materials metric definition, permit-aware evidence handling, field-risk escalation, and disclosure-safe boundary behavior.

Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all analytics-reporting-and-disclosure abilities, and both industrial-and-regulated-operations abilities required by the issue contract.
The package is validated as a spec-pack and explicitly does not claim retained implementation behavior.
